name: Nomad Bridge Free-For-All Drain
tldr: A routine upgrade to Nomad's Replica contract initialized the trusted Merkle root to 0x00, causing the message-proof check to auto-pass for any arbitrary message. Within ~2.5 hours, hundreds of copy-cat attackers drained ~$190M in a permissionless free-for-all.
coreContradiction: The contract accepted any message as 'proven' because the trusted root was the same zero value returned for any unmapped key, making forgery indistinguishable from legitimacy.

attackSteps.label: Bad initialize() sets root to 0x00
attackSteps.detail: A June 21 2022 upgrade to the Replica implementation called initialize() with committedRoot = bytes32(0), which wrote confirmAt[0x00] = 1 — marking the zero hash as a permanently trusted root.

attackSteps.label: acceptableRoot() returns true for any message
attackSteps.detail: process() calls acceptableRoot(messages[msgHash]). For any message never previously stored, messages[] returns the default bytes32(0), which confirmAt maps to 1 (block 1, already passed), so the check passes unconditionally.

attackSteps.label: First attacker crafts 100 WBTC withdrawal
attackSteps.detail: Exploiter 1 (0x56D8B635…) used a helper contract and Flashbots to privately submit a forged process() message instructing the ERC20 bridge to release 100 WBTC (~$2.3M) to their address in block 15259101.

attackSteps.label: Copy-cat replay drains remaining ~$188M
attackSteps.detail: Once the exploit tx appeared on-chain, anyone could copy the calldata, replace the recipient address, and replay it. Nearly 300 addresses drained the bridge across all ERC-20 tokens over ~150 minutes.

fundFlow.note: First exploit tx — canonical drain of 100 WBTC (~$2.3M) in block 15259101
Partial return by white-hat copy-cat exploiters after Nomad's recovery bounty
